extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution. Key facts: (1) opted_out becomes true only via t1, and NO transition ever sets opted_out back to false, so opted_out is monotonic. (2) The ONLY transition that sets sent := true is t2 (send_attempt), whose guard requires not opted_out; it is therefore disabled whenever opted_out is true. (3) The transition t1 that establishes opted_out := true atomically also sets sent := false, so the first state with opted_out true has sent = false. (4) Every transition that can fire while opted_out is true either sets sent := false (t1 optout; t33 send_attempt-when-opted-out; all key transitions t3–t32) or is disabled (t2). Hence, once opted_out is true, sent can only be set to false and never to true. By induction over execution length (covering the bounded 13-event horizon and indeed all horizons), no reachable state has opted_out true and sent true simultaneously. The invariant is never violated.
